preamble: WE, THE PEOPLE OF INDIA, having solemnly resolved to constitute India into a SOVEREIGN, SOCIALIST, SECULAR, DEMOCRATIC, REPUBLIC and to secure to all its citizens: JUSTICE, social, economic and political; LIBERTY of thought, expression, belief, faith and worship; EQUALITY of status and of opportunity; and to promote among them all FRATERNITY assuring the dignity of the individual and the unity and integrity of the Nation; IN OUR CONSTITUENT ASSEMBLY this twenty-sixth day of November, 1949, do HEREBY ADOPT, ENACT AND GIVE TO OURSELVES THIS CONSTITUTION.

parts.title: Relations between the Union and the States
Services under the Union and the States
Special Provisions Relating to Certain Classes
Short Title, Commencement, Authoritative Text in Hindi and Repeals

articles.title: Name and territory of the Union
articles.description: India, that is Bharat, shall be a Union of States.

articles.title: Equality before law
articles.description: The State shall not deny to any person equality before law or equal protection of the laws within the territory of India.

articles.title: Prohibition of discrimination
articles.description: The State shall not discriminate against any citizen on grounds of religion, race, caste, sex, place of birth.

articles.title: Equality of opportunity in public employment
articles.description: There shall be equality of opportunity for all citizens in matters of public employment.

articles.title: Abolition of Untouchability
articles.description: Untouchability is abolished and its practice in any form is forbidden.

articles.title: Abolition of titles
articles.description: No title, not being a military or academic distinction, shall be conferred by the State.

articles.title: Protection of certain rights regarding freedom of speech
articles.description: All citizens shall have the right to freedom of speech and expression, to assemble peaceably, to form associations, to move freely, to reside and settle in any part of the country, to practice any profession.

articles.title: Protection in respect of conviction for offences
articles.description: No person shall be punished for acts which were not offences when committed.

articles.title: Protection of life and personal liberty
articles.description: No person shall be deprived of life or personal liberty except according to procedure established by law.

articles.title: Right to education
articles.description: The State shall provide free and compulsory education to all children of 6 to 14 years.

articles.title: Protection against arrest and detention
articles.description: Every person who is arrested shall be produced before a magistrate within 24 hours.

articles.title: Prohibition of traffic in human beings
articles.description: Traffic in human beings and other similar forms of forced labour are prohibited.

articles.title: Prohibition of employment of children
articles.description: No child below 14 years shall be employed in factories or mines.

articles.title: Freedom of conscience and free profession
articles.description: All persons are equally entitled to freedom of conscience and right to freely profess, practice and propagate religion.

articles.title: Freedom to manage religious affairs
articles.description: Every religious denomination has right to establish institutions and to manage its own affairs.

articles.title: Freedom from taxes
articles.description: No person shall be compelled to pay taxes for propagation of any religion.

articles.title: Freedom from attendance at religious instruction
articles.description: No religious instruction shall be provided in public educational institutions.

articles.title: Protection of interests of minorities
articles.description: Any section of citizens with distinct language shall have right to conserve its language.

articles.title: Right of minorities to establish educational institutions
articles.description: All minorities shall have right to establish and administer educational institutions.

articles.title: Compulsory acquisition of property
articles.description: [Repealed by 44th Amendment]

articles.title: Remedies for enforcement of Fundamental Rights
articles.description: The right to move Supreme Court for enforcement of Fundamental Rights.

articles.title: Power of High Courts to issue writs
articles.description: Every High Court shall have power to issue writs for enforcement of Fundamental Rights.

articles.title: Power of President to grant pardons
articles.description: The President shall have power to grant pardons, reprieves, respites or remissions of punishment.

articles.title: Council of Ministers to aid and advise President
articles.description: There shall be a Council of Ministers to aid and advise the President.

articles.title: Power of High Courts to issue writs
articles.description: High Courts can issue writs for enforcement of Fundamental Rights.

articles.title: Functions of Public Service Commissions
articles.description: Public Service Commissions advise on matters relating to recruitment and disciplinary matters.

articles.title: Election Commission
articles.description: The Election Commission shall consist of Chief Election Commissioner and other members.

articles.title: Adult suffrage
articles.description: Every citizen who has attained the age of 21 years shall be entitled to vote.

articles.title: Bar to interference by courts
articles.description: No court shall question the validity of any election.

articles.title: Emergency provisions
articles.description: If the President is satisfied that there is a grave emergency, he may issue proclamation.

articles.title: President's Rule
articles.description: If President is satisfied that the state cannot be governed, he may assume to the state.

articles.title: Power of Parliament to amend Constitution
articles.description: Parliament may in exercise of its constituent power amend by way of addition, variation or repeal.
